message:
audit(btree): bd-9e3xf cache-miss classification rerun summary

Re-ran `audit_cell_slot_cache_hit_rate_write_vs_read` at HEAD (de40c3df)
after shipping the 7-commit CellRef-parse skip + inline-capacity family.
Full summary attached as a bead comment on bd-9e3xf; this commit is the
bead-sync flush so other agents see the new data.

Before (5392c91e baseline) → After (de40c3df):

index_random_insert:
  hits      64,416 →        0   (-64,416; see note 1 below)
  cold       6,366 →    7,439   (+1,073)
  inv        5,747 →   20,869   (+15,122)
  slot      54,739 →  102,960   (+48,221)
  total    131,268 →  131,268   (unchanged)
  hit_rate   49.07 % →   0.00 %

delete_all_after_append (unchanged):
  hits=98 cold=2 inv=9,998 slot=0  hit_rate=0.97 %

Three interpretations:

(1) The 49 % → 0 % drop on index_random_insert is an artefact of
    1be6ee30's key switch from xxh3_64(page_bytes) to
    PageData::image_token. MemPageStore allocates a fresh Vec (fresh
    image_token) on every `read_page_data`, so no two descents share
    a cache key even when the bytes are identical. The production
    pager preserves image_token via page-cache `Clone`, so the 49 %
    hit rate should carry over to real workloads.

(2) MISS_SLOT stays the dominant miss class on index_random_insert
    (78 % of misses), which is why the CachedCellSlots inline-capacity
    widen from 8 → 16 (also in 1be6ee30) still stands as the right
    lever for that pattern.

(3) The 5 CellRef::parse-skip commits (4b061dcc, 385591f8, b35f091c,
    145bfe36, de40c3df) don't change this MemPageStore audit's counts
    because index_random_insert exercises binary_search_index_{leaf,
    interior} — paths that still legitimately need the full parse.
    The skips pay off on DELETE-rebalance and split hot paths that
    aren't on the audit's critical path.

Full summary (6-scenario table + shipment ledger + raw counter output)
captured as bead comment on bd-9e3xf via `br comments add`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
